assessment_items.question_content:
Scientists have found evidence of five major mass extinction events during Earth’s history. One of these, the end-Permian extinction, resulted in the extinction of roughly 96% of Earth’s species. These species included many early reptiles, amphibians and the last species of trilobites. 

One cause of the end-Permian extinction was extreme volcanic activity that generated huge clouds of sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide. The presence of these gases in the atmosphere led to acid rain, ocean acidification, and global warming.

**Which of the following describes how the end-Permian extinction was similar to Earth’s other four major mass extinctions events?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The extinction was due to large-scale ecological changes.
The extinction affected trilobites, amphibians, and reptiles.
The extinction was caused primarily by human activity.
The extinction led to ocean acidification and global warming.

assessment_items.question_content:
Scientists have found evidence of five major mass extinction events during Earth’s history. One of these, the Cretaceous-Paleogene extinction, resulted in the extinction of roughly 76% of Earth’s species. These species included all of the non-avian dinosaurs. 

One cause of the Cretaceous-Paleogene extinction was an asteroid impact that sent immense amounts of dust, sulfur and other substances into the air. This led to ocean acidification and a rapid decrease in global temperatures.

**Which of the following describes how the Cretaceous-Paleogene extinction was similar to Earth’s other four major mass extinctions events?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The extinction was due to global environmental changes.
The extinction affected non-avian dinosaurs.
The extinction was caused primarily by human activity.
The extinction led to ocean acidification and global cooling.

assessment_items.question_content:
Scientists have found evidence of five major mass extinction events during Earth’s history. One of these, the Late Devonian extinction, resulted in the extinction of roughly 75% of Earth’s species. These included marine invertebrates and all species of armored fish.

One cause of the Late Devonian extinction was the rise of land plants which consumed carbon dioxide from the atmosphere and caused the release of nutrients into the oceans. This led to global cooling, and a reduction in ocean oxygen levels.

**Which of the following describes how the Late Devonian extinction was similar to Earth’s other four major mass extinctions events?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The extinction was due to widespread ecological changes.
The extinction affected marine invertebrates and armored fish.
The extinction was caused primarily by human activity.
The extinction led to global cooling and ocean oxygen depletion.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each diagram below shows a set of sedimentary rock layers in the fossil record. A thin, black layer represents an area rich in iridium from volcanic ash, indicating a time of extreme volcanic activity on Earth’s surface.
 
**Which of the following diagrams shows evidence of a mass extinction event caused by extreme volcanic activity on Earth’s surface?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: To identify the diagram that shows evidence of the mass extinction event, we need to consider both the order of the rock layers, and the pattern of fossils found within those layers.

A mass extinction event will appear as an abrupt decrease in species abundance and diversity from one layer to the next, going forward in time. Deeper rock layers are typically older than shallower rock layers. So, we’re looking for a diagram with many species in the deeper layer, and few or no species in the shallower layer.

Remember that we’re looking for the diagram that represents a mass extinction event caused by extreme volcanic activity on Earth’s surface. According to the text, this activity is represented by a thin, black layer. So, we’re looking for the diagram that has the thin, black layer *between* the layers showing the mass extinction event.

choices.content: ![The image is 3 layers and the top layer is yellow and has 2 identical objects in it. The middle layer is a thin black line, and the bottom layer is brown with 3 different types of objects in it and a total of 8 objects. On the left of the image is a vertical line with shallower written at the top next to the yellow layer and deeper written on the bottom next to the brown layer.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/76689a10fdf6e516b091c0a959f6e11a.png)

![The image is 3 layers. The top layer is brown and has nothing in it, the middle layer is yellow with 3 different types of objects in it and a total of 9 objects, and the bottom layer is a thin black line. On the left of the image is a vertical line with shallower written at the top next to the brown layer and deeper written on the bottom next to the thin black line.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/dc2811f6744f9f1e4e7d3e882fb83ee6.png)

![The image is 3 layers and the top layer is a think black line. The middle layer is yellow and has 2 identical objects in it, and the bottom layer is brown with 3 different types of objects in it and a total of 9 objects. On the left of the image is a vertical line with shallower written at the top next to the thin black line and  deeper written on the bottom next to the brown layer.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d94ec5200f1a7297e1d09996ae10d9d5.png)

![The image is 3 layers. The top layer is brown with 3 different types of objects in it and a total of 9 objects, the middle layer is a thin black line, and the bottom layer is yellow and has 2 identical objects in it. On the left of the image is a vertical line with shallower written at the top next to the brown layer and  deeper written on the bottom next to yellow layer.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5fa288a6c7bc031e0f77983c1808108f.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
Each diagram below shows a set of sedimentary rock layers in the fossil record. A thin, black layer represents an area rich in the rare metal iridium, which is evidence of a large meteor strike.

**Which of the following diagrams shows evidence of a mass extinction event caused by a large meteor strike?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: To identify the diagram that shows evidence of the mass extinction event, we need to consider both the order of the rock layers, and the pattern of fossils found within those layers.

A mass extinction event will appear as an abrupt decrease in species abundance and diversity from one layer to the next, going forward in time. Deeper rock layers are typically older than shallower rock layers. So, we’re looking for a diagram with many species in the deeper layer, and few or no species in the shallower layer.

Remember that we’re looking for the diagram that represents a mass extinction event caused by a large meteor strike. According to the text, this activity is represented by a thin, black layer. So, we’re looking for the diagram that has the thin, black layer *between* the layers showing the mass extinction event.

choices.content: ![The image is 3 layers and the top layer is light gray and has 2 identical objects in it. The middle layer is a thin black line, and the bottom layer is dark gray with 3 different types of objects in it and a total of 8 objects. On the left of the image is a vertical line with shallower written at the top of the gray layer and deeper written on the bottom of the dark gray layer.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/27f9d017cec5026d48aa5fbd608a1649.png)

![The image is 3 layers. The top layer is dark gray and has nothing in it, the middle layer is light gray with 3 different types of objects in it and a total of 9 objects, and the bottom layer is a thin black line. On the left of the image is a vertical line with shallower written at the top of the dark gray layer and deeper written on the bottom next to the thin black line.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5df3a882144c1cf1940c148dea293845.png)

![The image is 3 layers and the top layer is a think black line. The middle layer is light gray and has 2 identical objects in it, and the bottom layer is dark gray with 3 different types of objects in it and a total of 9 objects. On the left of the image is a vertical line with shallower written at the top next to the thin black line and  deeper written on the bottom of the dark gray layer.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f491405f8a80b40dd01c5e086cd9f197.png)

![The image is 3 layers. The top layer is dark gray with 3 different types of objects in it and a total of 9 objects, the middle layer is a thin black line, and the bottom layer is light gray and has 2 identical objects in it. On the left of the image is a vertical line with shallower written at the top next to the dark gray layer and  deeper written on the bottom next to the light gray layer.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/68f64ab986a695b3e762955d989049b4.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
Each diagram below shows a set of sedimentary rock layers in the fossil record. A thin, black layer represents an area containing shocked quartz, which is evidence of a large meteor strike.

**Which of the following diagrams shows evidence of a mass extinction event caused by a large meteor strike?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: To identify the diagram that shows evidence of the mass extinction event, we need to consider both the order of the rock layers, and the pattern of fossils found within those layers.

A mass extinction event will appear as an abrupt decrease in species abundance and diversity from one layer to the next, going forward in time. Deeper rock layers are typically older than shallower rock layers. So, we’re looking for a diagram with many species in the deeper layer, and few or no species in the shallower layer.

Remember that we’re looking for the diagram that represents a mass extinction event caused by a large meteor strike. According to the text, this activity is represented by a thin, black layer. So, we’re looking for the diagram that has the thin, black layer *between* the layers showing the mass extinction event.

The middle layer is a thin black line, and the bottom layer is brown with 3 different types of objects in it and a total of 8 objects. On the left of the image is a vertical line with shallower written at the top of the tan box and deeper written on the bottom of the brown layer.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b5ff38462ad8c910d2602212fe3769be.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
The following graph shows the number of tetrapod (four-limbed animal) families on Earth over time, as indicated by the fossil record. The arrows indicate major mass extinction events.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following statements is consistent with the information provided in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The y axis of the line graph is labeled Number of tetrapod families and is marked at intervals of 50, 100, 150, 200, 250, 300 and 350. The x axis is labeled million years ago and is marked at intervals of 300, 250, 200, 150, 100, 50 and 0. The line begins at the intersection of the x and y axis and rises gradually. There are 3 arrows labeling different points on the line. The first arrow is at 250, 50 and is marked end-Permian extinction. The second arrow is at 200, 75 and is marked end-Triassic extinction. The third arrow is at 50, 150 and is marked end-Cretaceous extinction. After the third arrow there is a rapid vertical rise of the line, that continues until the end of the graph at 0, 350.

choices.content: Tetrapods underwent an adaptive radiation after the end-Cretaceous extinction, likely due to the use of ecological niches made available by the extinction event.
The tetrapod families that were lost during the end-Permian extinction returned after a lag of a few million years as they re-populated previously occupied habitats.
The extinction rate between the end-Permian and the end-Triassic extinctions indicates this was a time of global ecological stress.
Since the end-Permian extinction, the number of tetrapod families on Earth has halved roughly every 100 million years.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following graph shows species number over time for a certain class of organisms. A mass extinction event is indicated by an arrow.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following statements is most consistent with the information provided in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The y axis of the line graph is labeled number of species, and the x axis is labeled Time, millions of years. The line on the graph begins at approximately ¼ of the y axis line and continues to rise diagonally upward. At the midpoint of the x axis there is a sharp dip in the line with an arrow pointing to the dip and the label Mass extinction. After that drop, there is a sharp, almost vertical rise of the line that continues until the end of the graph.

choices.content: The organisms underwent an adaptive radiation after the mass extinction event, likely due to the use of newly available ecological niches.
The species that were lost during the mass extinction event returned after a lag of a few million years as they re-populated previously occupied habitats.
Before the mass extinction event began, the extinction rate was greater than the speciation rate, likely due to ecological stress.
The class of organisms represented in the graph has reached a threshold number of species and will likely experience another mass extinction event as a result.

assessment_items.question_content:
Roughly 66 million years ago, the Cretaceous-Paleogene mass extinction event caused all non-avian dinosaurs and some mammal groups to go extinct. In the 10 million years following the extinction event, roughly 4,000 new species of mammals evolved, including the first fully aquatic mammals and flying mammals.

**Which of the following statements is most consistent with the information provided in the text above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Mammals underwent an adaptive radiation after the mass extinction event, likely due to the use of newly available ecological niches.
The mammal groups that were lost during the mass extinction event returned during the next 10 million years as they re-populated previously occupied habitats.
After the mass extinction event, the mammalian speciation rate was less than the extinction rate, likely due to ecological stress.
The number of mammal species has greatly increased since the mass extinction event, putting mammals in danger of another mass extinction event.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following does *not* describe a human activity that is likely to contribute to extinction in a coral reef ecosystem?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A government establishes a marine protected area that restricts the types of activities that can be carried out in and around the coral reef.
Lawmakers remove environmental regulations on greenhouse gas emissions, leading to an increase in ocean temperatures over time.
Certain fishers practice dynamite fishing, which destroys coral reefs and harms off-target species.
A SCUBA diving instructor doesn’t educate students in best practices, resulting in divers accidentally kicking and breaking corals.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following does *not* describe a human activity that is likely to contribute to extinction in a temperate forest ecosystem?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A nonprofit organization plants millions of trees to help restore logged North American forests.
Lawmakers remove environmental regulations on greenhouse gas emissions, leading to prolonged drought year after year.
A plant nursery provides trees for forest planting that are unintentionally infected with a disease-causing fungus.
A developer builds houses and roads in a forested area, cutting down many trees in the process.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following does *not* describe a human activity that is likely to contribute to extinction in a river ecosystem?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A group of organizations works together to remove a dam, opening up miles of river for spawning fish.
Lawmakers remove environmental regulations on greenhouse gas emissions, leading to decreased rainfall and a portion of the river drying out.
A farmer uses large amounts of fertilizer, which washes into a nearby river and causes algal blooms and anoxic water conditions.
A developer cuts down vegetation along a riverbank to build houses, decreasing shade and increasing water temperatures.
